capstone-domain-model.label: Capstone Domain Model
capstone-domain-model.summary: Build the final app domain classes with encapsulation, inheritance, overriding, and polymorphic labels.

sketch1.title: Capstone Domain Model: the file shape

sketch2.title: Capstone Domain Model: what the grader checks

try_capstone_domain_model_sketch1.title: Try it yourself
try_capstone_domain_model_sketch1.prompt: Create subclasses that override `label()` with different prefixes.

ci-capstone-domain-practice.title: Process mixed capstone tasks
ci-capstone-domain-practice.prompt: Complete a report service that accepts mixed task objects and calls `label()` on each.
ci-capstone-domain-practice.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

ci-capstone-domain-practice.starterCode:
from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask
from services.reports import task_lines

tasks = [BugTask("Crash", "high"), FeatureTask("Export", "reports")]
for line in task_lines(tasks):
    print(line)

sc-capstone-domain-model-design.title: Check the design choice
sc-capstone-domain-model-design.prompt: Which choice best matches the lesson goal for capstone domain model?
sc-capstone-domain-model-design.hint: Choose the option that keeps object responsibilities clear.

help.concept: Build the final app domain classes with encapsulation, inheritance, overriding, and polymorphic labels.
help.hint_1: Look for the answer that reduces duplication without hiding behavior.
help.hint_2: A good OOP answer usually keeps behavior near the object that owns the data.

options.a: Put every class in main.py so imports are never needed.
options.b: Give each class or service a focused responsibility and import it where needed.
options.c: Avoid methods so every object exposes all of its data directly.
options.d: Copy the same logic into each subclass to avoid shared code.

try-capstone-domain-model-sketch0.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-capstone-domain-model-sketch0.starterCode:
from models.base_task import BaseTask

task = BaseTask("Plan", "open")
task.mark_done()
print(task.label())

try-capstone-domain-model-sketch1.prompt: Create subclasses that override `label()` with different prefixes.
try-capstone-domain-model-sketch1.hint: Read the imports first, then put each responsibility in the file named by that import.

help.concept: In a multi-file project, each file should own one clear responsibility and imports should connect those responsibilities.
help.hint_1: Run from the entry file and follow the first failing import or assertion.
help.hint_2: Keep the full project state working, not just the one line you changed.

try-capstone-domain-model-sketch1.starterCode:
from models.bug_task import BugTask
from models.feature_task import FeatureTask

items = [BugTask("Fix login", "high"), FeatureTask("Add export", "reports")]
for item in items:
    print(item.label())

concept.title: Capstone Domain Model: the idea
concept.bodyMarkdown: **Capstone Domain Model** is not just syntax. In this course it is practiced inside small projects where one file owns one clear part of the program. Build the final app domain classes with encapsulation, inheritance, overriding, and polymorphic labels.

multifile-shape.title: Capstone Domain Model: the file shape
multifile-shape.bodyMarkdown: The file layout matters. Models hold object state and behavior, services coordinate groups of objects, tests verify behavior, and `main.py` stays thin enough to read.

graded-habit.title: Capstone Domain Model: what the grader checks
graded-habit.bodyMarkdown: The grader checks behavior from the running project. A strong answer keeps imports working, keeps all files present, and proves the object behavior through output or assertions.
